sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: In civil engineering construction such as railways, highways, water conservancy and buildings, the problem of expansive soil is sometimes encountered. The repeated effect of expansive soil expanding when it meets water and contracting when it loses water may cause serious damage to the structures built on it. To minimize the lifting of structures caused by the expansion of expansive soil upon contact with water, the foundation of structures often adopts the form of pile foundation. Due to the complexity of expansive soil itself, the interaction mechanism between piles and expansive soil is quite different from the general pile-soil interaction mechanism. When the design or construction is improper, the piles will be subjected to the upward pulling force caused by the expansion of expansive soil, leading to the lifting of the piles along with the structures on them, and even resulting in the breakage of the piles and other destructive phenomena, seriously affecting the safety of the structures. Therefore, conducting in-depth research on the pile-soil interaction in expansive soil foundations has significant theoretical value and practical engineering significance. Based on a comprehensive analysis of the current research status of piles in expansive soil at home and abroad, this paper adopts a research method combining indoor tests, simulation tests, theoretical research and numerical analysis, and has achieved the following main research results and conclusions: Through a large number of indoor expansion and contraction tests on expansive soil in the Tan Shao Expressway of Hunan Province and the Nanning area of Guangxi, the expansion and contraction characteristics of expansive soil were systematically studied. The regression equations of expansion force and expansion rate were established, providing a basis for predicting and estimating the differential uplift or settlement of expansive soil foundations. (2) Based on the research on the expansion and contraction characteristics of expansive soil, a new method for calculating the expansion and uplift of homogeneous expansive soil foundations and layered expansive soil foundations is proposed. This method can not only calculate the final values of expansion and uplift at each depth in the foundation, but also predict the changes of uplift over time. By comparing with the test results, it is proved that it has good reliability and practicability. (3) In the research on the load transfer of expansive soil foundation piles, the expansion and contraction effects of expansive soil were taken into account, and an analytical calculation method for the load transfer of foundation piles under the combined effects of the load at the top of the pile and the expansion or contraction of expansive soil was proposed. Due to the introduction of the time effect of soil expansion and contraction, this method can predict the variation laws of the axial force of the pile shaft, deformation and the frictional resistance on the pile side with time. Through comparative analysis with three examples, it is found that this method is reliable and has the advantages of simplicity, strong practicability and high calculation accuracy. (4) Through the analysis of a large amount of direct shear friction test data at the contact surface between expansive soil and concrete blocks, a constitutive model for the mechanical analysis of the pile-expansive soil contact surface is proposed, which is a supplement to the existing model. The five parameters involved in this model can be obtained through direct shear friction tests. (5) Indoor simulation tests were conducted on the pile-soil interaction in expansive soil foundations. The vertical static load characteristics of the foundation piles in expansive soil foundations and the influence of water immersion expansion of expansive soil on the foundation piles were systematically studied, providing experimental and theoretical basis for further numerical simulation analysis of the pile-soil interaction. (6) Based on the theory of fluid-solid coupling mechanics, the process of water absorption expansion or water loss contraction of expansive soil is regarded as a dynamic coupling process. A seepage - deformation coupling model is established, and the relevant theory of unsaturated expansive soil is introduced. A fluid-solid coupling finite element program for unsaturated expansive soil with good open function is compiled in FORTRAN language. (7) In the above-mentioned fluid-solid coupling finite element program for unsaturated expansive soil, a pile-soil analysis module was added to conduct fluid-solid coupling analysis on the pile-soil interaction in expansive soil foundations. In the analysis, the Goodman joint element was selected as the pile-expansive soil interface element, and the constitutive model of the pile-expansive soil contact surface established in this paper was introduced. By comparing with examples, the feasibility and correctness of the calculation method and the selected model have been verified. Based on the aforementioned work, numerical simulation analysis was conducted on the pile-soil interaction in expansive soil foundations. The influence of factors such as pile length, pile diameter, cap, bottom expansion, and pile-soil interface characteristics on the pile-soil interaction mechanism in expansive soil foundations was systematically and emphatically studied.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: Expansive soil is a special type of regional clay that is widely distributed in China. With the extensive construction of high-grade highways in our country, the problems of expansive soil subgrade filling and slope stability have become increasingly prominent. Therefore, the research on it has significant practical significance. This paper, by means of experimental analysis and theoretical analysis, focuses on studying issues such as the influence of lime dosage on the properties of lime-improved expansive soil, the properties of ctmab-improved expansive soil, and the calculation methods for the expansion and contraction deformation of expansive soil foundations. The main content is as follows: Firstly, the research results on the basic engineering properties of lime-modified expansive soil and the mechanism of lime-modified expansive soil are summarized. Through laboratory tests, it is indicated that compacted expansive soil cannot be used as subgrade fill soil, while the expansion and contraction characteristics, strength and water stability of lime-modified expansive soil have been significantly improved compared with plain soil and can be used as subgrade fill soil. Compaction tests were conducted on plain soil and lime-improved soil to study the variation relationships of compaction curves, maximum dry density and optimal moisture content with lime dosage. The variation relationships of indicators such as free expansion rate, liquid plastic limit, and total expansion and contraction rate with ash dosage were studied through indoor experiments. Experimental studies were conducted on the variation of the expansion rate of plain soil and lime improved soil with the overburden pressure under different compaction degrees. In order to explore the variation of moisture content in different parts of the roadbed with the seasons, moisture meters were buried in different parts of the roadbed to study the influence of seasonal rainfall and groundwater level on the moisture content of the fill soil layer. In order to analyze the water stability of lime-improved soil, this paper conducted a dry-wet cycle comparative test on lime-improved soil. The results show that the water stability of lime-improved soil has been significantly enhanced compared with plain soil. Lime can be used to improve the filling material of expansive soil fill slopes, but it cannot be used to improve the soil properties of natural expansive soil slopes. Therefore, this paper attempts to use CTMAB as an improver to improve expansive soil and briefly expounds the mechanism of CTMAB in soil improvement. The relationship between the free expansion rate, expansion force and cyclic expansion deformation characteristics of CTMAB improved soil and the addition amount of CTMAB was studied through indoor experiments. Meanwhile, the photos of the samples after dry and wet cycling of plain soil and CTMAB improved soil were compared and analyzed. The research shows that CTMAB can be used to improve the soil properties of natural expansive soil slopes and increase slope stability. Finally, this paper analyzes and compares the existing calculation methods for the expansion and contraction deformation of expansive soil foundations, and examines the mechanical conditions of the expansion and contraction deformation of expansive soil foundations. The expansion and contraction deformation of expansive soil foundations caused by changes in moisture content is estimated by experimental methods. Meanwhile, expansion force tests are conducted on soil with different moisture contents, and the subgrade filling without expansion deformation of expansive soil foundations is obtained Height.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: The mineral chemical composition of expansive soil is one of its most important characteristics research results have proved that the engineering properties of expansive soil are closely related to its mineral chemical composition, especially the mineral chemical composition of its clay aggregate part. It is precisely due to the physical-chemical interaction between the clay aggregate part and water that the properties of expansion and contraction occur. Studying the mineral chemical composition of expansive soil not only helps to understand the internal factors controlling the engineering characteristics of expansive soil, explore its expansion mechanism, and more effectively improve and reinforce expansive soil foundations, but is also indispensable for introducing physical-chemical parameters into the theoretical calculation of soil mechanics and exploring new technologies and methods for the research of expansive soil. At present, the main methods for identifying the mineral composition of expansive soil include differential thermal analysis, X-ray diffraction, infrared spectroscopy, and scanning electron microscopy, among other analytical means and methods. The main research methods for the chemical composition of expansive soil include total chemical analysis and trace element analysis, among others. Shu Fuhua systematically studied the influence of the mineral composition and chemical characteristics of expansive soil on its swelling and shrinking properties. He believed that the content of montmorillonite and the cation exchange capacity were the main internal factors affecting the swelling and shrinking performance of expansive soil, and the increase in the content of montmorillonite would lead to an increase in the swelling performance of expansive soil. Et al. studied the relationship between the mineral composition and the expansion potential of expansive soil rocks in Oman and also pointed out that the higher the content of montmorillonite, the greater the expansion potential accordingly. Tian Zhao studied the mineral composition of expansive soil in the United Arab Emirates by using thermodynamic models and X-ray diffraction methods, thereby revealing the reasons for the decline in the bearing capacity of expansive soil foundations. Yang Heping et al. conducted a relatively comprehensive soil property test on the expansive soil of the Chu-da Highway landslide analysis and proposed a new index of deformation modulus of expansive soil during the deformation process, obtaining the parameters for directly filling the embankment with expansive soil.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: Structure is another important factor influencing the engineering properties of expansive soil. The structure of expansive soil mainly refers to the macroscopic and microscopic structural characteristics closely related to its mechanical strength. The main feature of the macroscopic structure is the multi-fissure property of expansive soil. During the soil formation process of expansive soil, due to temperature, humidity, compaction and uneven expansion and contraction effects, many network-like interlaced cracks and weak structural planes are formed, thus endowing expansive soil with a series of unique mechanical properties. Lu Zaihua et al. conducted triaxial shear tests on undisturbed expansive soil in Nanyang under different suction forces and found that primary fractures and weak surfaces are the main factors determining the strength of undisturbed expansive soil. Wang Guoqiang found through the research on the engineering properties of expansive soil in the Jianghuai region of Anhui Province that due to the existence of fissures, the integrity of the expansive soil was damaged, which made the strength evaluation difficult; it has non-uniformity and variability, resulting in a significant difference between the strength determined by the shear test and the actual soil strength. Geng Jianbin classified expansive soil fractures into primary fractures and secondary fractures based on their causes and focused on discussing the factors influencing the formation and development of secondary fractures. With the application of fractal geometry theory, Yi Shunmin et al. explored the quantitative model of expansive soil fracture research from the perspective of combining fractal geometry with the study of expansive soil fracture structure. The microstructure of expansive soil refers to the microstructure characteristics formed by the arrangement of soil particles and particle clusters, the size, shape, quantity and spatial distribution of micro-pores and micro-fissures, as well as the filling conditions, contact and connection methods, etc., under certain geological environment and conditions. The microstructure of expansive soil is also one of the important factors affecting the engineering properties of expansive soil. As early as that year, the founder of soil mechanics proposed that when evaluating the engineering properties of soil, importance must be attached to the study of soil microstructure. At the end of the century, advanced electron technologies such as scanning electron microscopy and transmission electron microscopy were applied to the field of soil microstructure research, which initiated a breakthrough in soil structure research. The year was a prosperous period for soil microstructure research, and a special international conference on microstructure was held in that year. In the 1980s, Wang Youlin et al. and Gao Guorui conducted extensive research on the relationship between clay mineral lamelles and their engineering properties. Since the 1980s, Liao Shiwen, Li Shenglin, Chuan Shi Bin, Yi Diao and others have introduced various measurement methods for the microstructure of cohesive soil and, through the study of the microstructure of expansive soil, have concluded that the swelling and contraction property, strength characteristics and deformation characteristics of expansive soil largely depend on the structural characteristics of expansive soil. Tan Luorong et al. studied the microstructure characteristics of some typical expansive soils in China, discussed the relationship between microstructure characteristics and expansive potential, and established the relationship between the orientation characteristics of microstructure and mechanical strength. Nowadays, due to the development of electronic image processing systems, it plays a certain role in the composition characteristics and formation mechanism of soil, as well as in the estimation and prediction of soil engineering properties and the establishment of constitutive relations by applying structural analysis results. In conclusion, microstructure research has now developed to the quantitative stage. The current issue is how quantitative research can develop towards simplified testing methods for engineering use.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: When conducting engineering construction in expansive soil areas, it is first necessary to accurately identify expansive soil from non-expansive soil. Then, the expansive soil should be classified, that is, expansive soil with basically similar engineering properties should be divided into the same type of expansive soil, while expansive soil with significantly different engineering properties should be classified into different categories, so as to provide reasonable parameters and scientific basis for the design and construction of the project. At present, many indicators for identifying expansive soil have been proposed at home and abroad, and opinions remain divided to this day. The indicators used for identifying expansive soil can be roughly classified into two major categories: one reflects the natural structure and state of the soil, and the other reflects the interaction between the material composition of the soil and water. The first category of indicators requires the determination of undisturbed soil samples, and its determination method is more restricted by conditions. The second category of indicators reflects the basic characteristics of soil particles, which can be measured by disturbing the sample. The determination conditions are simple and easy to implement, so it is mostly adopted. In recent years, many scholars at home and abroad have conducted extensive research on the classification of expansive soil and proposed many classification methods. Among them, the representative classification methods are as follows: ① Classification based on the maximum expansion and contraction index ② Classification based on the free expansion rate and the total expansion and contraction rate ③ Classification method based on multivariate linear function discrimination ④ Classification is carried out based on clay content, liquid limit and linear expansion and contraction rate, as well as specific surface area and ion exchange capacity ⑤ Classification is based on the plasticity diagram ⑥ Classification is based on free expansion rate and foundation deformation ⑦ India uses multiple indicators such as liquid limit, plasticity index, shrinkage index, clay content, expansion potential, expansion rate, and free expansion rate to classify black cotton soil ⑧ Williams of South Africa proposed the combined use of plasticity index and content of smaller particles for graphical classification method ⑨ Holtz, United States Bureau of Reclamation, proposed a classification method based on the indicators of colloidal particle content, plasticity index, shrinkage limit and expansion body transformation ⑩ The classification method of air-drying moisture content proposed by Chinese scholars Gong index. With the application of methods such as fuzzy mathematics, grey theory, and artificial neural networks in geotechnical engineering, the comprehensive discrimination and classification of expansive soil using multiple indicators have increasingly attracted people's attention. Methods such as grey correlation method, grey clustering method, fuzzy mathematics discrimination method, and classification method based on artificial neural networks have emerged. They all adopted multiple indicators for discrimination and classification and used weights to describe the importance of each indicator for the discrimination and classification of expansive soil, achieving very good results. In addition, Jia Dongliang also achieved good discrimination results in the classification of expansive soil by using the unascertained measure model. Ding Jiaming et al. provided a new method for the classification of expansive soil by combining Bayesian theory with decision mining of incompatible systems based on rough sets. For the classification of expansive soil, if a single one of the above-mentioned discrimination methods is adopted, it may lead to discrimination errors. It is best to use multiple discrimination methods for comprehensive discrimination and classification to achieve the best discrimination and classification effect.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: Expansive soil is mainly composed of some hydrophilic minerals such as montmorillonite, illite and kaolinite and shows the characteristics of water absorption, swelling and softening, water loss, dry shrinkage and cracking, that is, it undergoes intense swelling and shrinking deformation. Theoretical research on the expansion and contraction mechanism of expansive soil is an extremely complex task. From phenomenological observation to theoretical analysis, predecessors have made tremendous efforts and proposed various theories and solution methods, such as the lattice expansion theory of clay minerals in expansive soil, the expansion theory of rheological constitutive equations proposed by Professors Chen Zongji and Sun Jun, and the humidity stress field theory proposed by Miao Xiexing et al. The double electric layer theory, the suction potential theory, the one-dimensional expansion theory, the three-dimensional expansion theory, Liu Zhenming's mechanical analysis theory based on the expansion and contraction path equation, the temperature stress field theory, the penetration theory, the elastic theory, the surface tension theory, the expansion potential theory, the free energy change theory, and the clay mineral lamellar interaction theory and elastic bending theory, etc., were proposed. However, none of these theories can provide a satisfactory explanation for the deformation mechanism and engineering effects of expansive soil. As stated in the literature, the reason why soil expands and contracts is that, first, there must be an internal cause for the soil itself to expand and contract, and second, there must be an external condition for water transfer. Both are indispensable. As for expansive soil as a whole, or for a certain regional expansive soil, or even for the same layer of expansive soil, the factors that can expand and contract itself are not exactly the same, and the external conditions in which expansive soil is located are even more diverse. Therefore, the expansion and contraction properties of expansive soil are not completely consistent, the processes by which it causes expansion and contraction phenomena are also different, and the results of expansion and contraction deformation also have significant differences. In response to this situation, Liu Tehong and Lin Tianjian attempted to integrate, integrate and modify the various current theories describing the expansion and contraction mechanism of expansive soil rocks and their engineering effects into a complete theoretical system—the theory of expansive soil engineering structure system. The deformation of expansive soil can be divided into two major categories: one is the compressive deformation under the action of external loads; the other is the wet expansion and wetting deformation under the combined action of external loads and infiltration or immersion, or the dry shrinkage deformation under the combined action of external loads and evaporation, air-drying, and water level drop. The study of the deformation characteristics of expansive soil is one of the important research contents of expansive soil and also the key to related engineering prevention and control. It is necessary to analyze and establish a new constitutive model of unsaturated expansive soil that reflects the characteristics of wet expansion, wet subsidence and dry shrinkage based on a large number of laboratory tests and engineering examples in order to describe the deformation characteristics of expansive soil more accurately. Based on this idea, Huang Gengzu studied the qualitative law of expansive deformation through expansive deformation experiments. Liu Zude et al. investigated the three-dimensional deformation characteristics of expansive soil under different initial water content conditions under the action of three-dimensional stress states, discussed the influence of various factors on it. Sun Changlong studied the deformation law of expansive soil foundation using load plate experiments. Lei Shengyou et al. studied the influence of dynamic load frequency on the deformation of expansive soil and established a mathematical model for the long-term deformation of reconstructed expansive soil under cyclic loading. Wang Yuan conducted a systematic study and analysis on the three-directional and unidirectional deformation performance of expansive soil under complex stress conditions, proposed some practical deformation calculation formulas and design calculation parameters, and generalized and described the extremely complex expansion and contraction deformation performance of expansive soil in a relatively simple form and parameters. Xu Yongfu et al., based on the expansion deformation data of expansive soil with different moisture contents and dry densities under different pressures, studied the correlation between the expansion amount and expansion force of expansive soil and the initial moisture content, dry density and pressure, and proposed the calculation formula for the expansion amount of expansive soil foundation. Liu Songyu et al. studied the influence of dry-wet cycling on the expansion and contraction characteristics of compacted expansive soil and found that the expansion and contraction deformation is not completely reversible. With the development of dry-wet cycling, the expansion rate of expansive soil increases. The absolute expansion rate always increases while the relative expansion rate decreases. Miao Linchang et al. conducted an expansion test of indoor expansive soil samples to study the relationship between the expansion deformation of different compacted expansive soil samples under different pressures and the initial moisture content and dry density of the soil samples. Su Long et al. studied the correlation between the expansion amount of expansive soil in Ningxia and its moisture content and pressure, and established the regression equation of the expansion amount and pressure of expansive soil in Ningxia. Xu Yongfu, by applying the rate process theory, concluded that the expansion deformation of unsaturated expansive soil is a function of time. Han Huaqiang et al. studied the deformation characteristics of expansive soil under dry-wet cycling and found that dry-wet cycling would cause changes in the soil structure of unsaturated expansive soil, thereby significantly reducing its deformation modulus. In addition, the author and his research group conducted a large number of experiments and theoretical studies on the deformation of expansive soil and also obtained some beneficial conclusions.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: The disasters of expansive soil are mainly caused by its water loss shrinkage and water absorption expansion. Uneven contraction causes soil to crack, destroying the integrity of the soil, and water absorption and expansion reduce the density of the soil; both can reduce the strength of expansive soil, resulting in a decrease in the bearing capacity of the foundation. In addition, intense and restricted expansion and contraction can cause tensile damage to buildings, etc. From the investigation of some cases of damage to light buildings in expansive land areas, it was found that the walls, ground beams and concrete floors of buildings cracked and were damaged due to the expansion and contraction of the foundation. The reason was that the expansive land foundation was exposed to the atmosphere during construction and lost water. After covering it with an impermeable cover layer, it caused excessive expansion due to the continuous absorption of water from the surrounding, especially after rain, by the expansive soil. Differential expansion causes damage to the covering, and this kind of damage often has the characteristics of multiple recurrence and long-term potential danger. Therefore, it is very important to study the expansion and contraction characteristics of expansive soil. The reasons for the expansion and contraction of expansive soil are very complex. It is the result of the combined action of the special internal factors of expansive soil under appropriate external environmental conditions. That is to say, the reason why expansive soil expands and contracts is that it must have the internal factors that enable the soil itself to expand and contract. It mainly depends on factors such as the mineral composition, chemical composition, cation exchange capacity and structural type of expansive soil. The second is that there must be external conditions for water transfer.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: Expansive force refers to the maximum stress generated after water absorption when the volume expansion of expansive soil is restricted. It can directly affect the working condition of structures in expansive soil areas, such as tensile stress in concrete piles, cracks in concrete linings of channels, and overturning of retaining walls, etc. The expansion force is measured by the equilibrium loading method. After installing the sample in accordance with the test standard and pouring pure water into the container from bottom to top, keeping the water level higher than the top surface of the sample, when the dial indicator starts to rotate clockwise, it indicates that the sample begins to expand. Immediately apply an appropriate balancing load to bring the dial indicator pointer back to its original position. When the applied load is sufficient to cause deformation of the instrument, when the next level of balancing load is applied, the pointer of the dial indicator should rotate counterclockwise by a value equal to the deformation of the instrument. When the specimen no longer expands at intervals under a certain level of load, it is considered that the specimen has reached a stable state under that level of load; the allowable expansion amount should be greater than. Based on the test data, the relationship curves between expansive force and dry density of the expansive soil samples from the Tanshao Expressway are shown in Figure 1, and the corresponding relationship curves between expansive force and moisture content are also shown in Figure 1. During the experiment, it could be observed that when kinds of expansive soil samples were immersed in water and expanded under confined conditions, the expansive force increased significantly from a small amount at the beginning, then increased slowly, and after several hours, it tended to stabilize and reached the maximum expansive force. It can be seen from the figure that for each type of expansive soil, it shows the same trend. The relationship between expansive force and dry density increases exponentially, that is, the expansive force increases with the increase of dry density. The relationship between expansive force and moisture content decreases exponentially, that is, it decreases with the increase of moisture content. The order in which the expansion force of the seed soil sample is significantly affected by dry density and moisture content is ‘soil sample - conventional soil sample’ soil sample.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: The causes and mechanisms of deformation in expansive soil: the internal factors mainly depend on the material composition of the expansive soil and its spatial structure characteristics in the soil; both material composition and microstructure characteristics are the basis for the expansion and contraction deformation of expansive soil. External factors depend on the environment and compaction conditions of the expansive soil, such as moisture content and dry density, as well as the additional loads it is subjected to. The literature summarizes that the water absorption and swelling of expansive soil is actually the result of a transformation from complex physical and chemical actions to mechanical actions at the contact surface between the clay mineral particles of expansive soil. Finally, the wedging effect of the water film pushes the particles away, increasing the distance between particles and causing the volume of the soil to expand. That is, the reason why expansive soil absorbs water and expands is the result of the physicochemical-mechanical effects produced by the interaction between expansive clay and water.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: Under the condition of applying the same compressive stress to the soil sample, the greater the initial moisture content, the smaller the final expansion of the soil sample when it is submerged and deforms stably, and vice versa. From the perspective of energy, the following analysis can be made: When the soil sample is prepared at a certain moisture content, the expansive minerals in the soil expand upon contact with water, and part of the expansion energy has already been exerted and consumed. When the sample is immersed in water again, the total expansion after stabilization is the exertion of the remaining expansion energy. For soil samples with high water content, their particle content is relatively low, the number of particles that come into contact with water and undergo complex physical and chemical reactions is small, the potential for expansion is small, the remaining expansion energy is relatively low, the final expansion amount is small, and the reverse is also true. The application of different stress states to soil samples indicates that the higher the stress level, the smaller the expansion of the soil sample after immersion in water. This is because the external force has a certain inhibitory effect on the exertion and development of the expansive force, and the work done by the external force consumes part of the expansive energy during the expansion process of the expansive soil. Therefore, the actual expansive energy used to cause the expansion deformation of the soil is not much. Macroscopically, it is manifested as a small amount of soil expansion, and vice versa.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: Expansive soil is a kind of geological body with many fissures and significant swelling and contracting properties. The repeated expansion upon contact with water and contraction upon loss of water in expansive rock and soil have extremely serious consequences for tunnel engineering. Therefore, it is necessary to obtain the mechanism of action when tunnels pass through expansive soil and the law of stress redistribution during expansion through research, and find corresponding countermeasures, so as to guide the design and construction of expansive soil tunnel structures. This paper, in combination with the project 'Research on the Structural Design and Construction Method of Expansive Soil Tunnels' of the Second Design Institute of the Ministry of Railways, mainly conducts a numerical simulation study on the effect of expansive soil distributed around the tunnel after absorbing water and swelling on the lining. Firstly, the causes and mechanisms of expansion and contraction of expansive soil, as well as the calculation methods of expansive soil tunnels, were introduced. It also introduces the basic characteristics of expansive soil surrounding rock, common diseases of expansive soil tunnels and their prevention and control measures. Then, through finite element numerical simulation, the effects of water absorption and expansion of the surrounding expansive soil on the tunnel lining and anchor rods after tunnel excavation were analyzed. The distribution laws of the internal forces of the lining, surface displacement and peri-tunnel displacement under different conditions were obtained, and the following conclusions were drawn: (1) The internal forces of the tunnel lining are related to the burial depth, expansion rate and thickness of the expansion ring. Among them, the expansion factor has a very significant influence on the internal force of the lining, while the burial depth factor has a relatively small influence. (2) For a certain anchor bolt, the maximum axial force before and after expansion occurs at the connection between the anchor bolt and the lining, and the axial force of the anchor bolt decreases from the inside to the outside along the axial direction of the anchor bolt. The axial force of the anchor rods at the waist of the arch is the greatest, while that at the top of the arch is the smallest. (3) The tensile force of the anchor bolt has a linear relationship with its expansion rate. Under the condition of a constant expansion rate, the thickness of the expansion ring has little influence on the maximum axial force of the anchor bolt. (4) The influence of the thickness and expansion rate of the expansion ring on the deformation of the lining is much greater than that of the burial depth. (5) After the expansion rings around the tunnel expand, the surface of the arch top rises upward. The degree of influence of burial depth on the surface displacement above the arch top increases with the increase of burial depth. (6) The expansion rate has the greatest impact on the tunnel structure, followed by the thickness of the expansion ring, and the burial depth has the least impact on the tunnel structure.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: In this paper, the expansion force, expansion rate, shrinkage rate, suction force and expansion and contraction cycle tests were conducted on the strongly expansive soil in Chenggong area, Yunnan Province and the weakly expansive soil in Meishan, Sichuan Province respectively by using the developed and improved expansion force and expansion rate test device, the multi-hole scale plate device and the π ruler device. The variation laws between them and the moisture content were obtained. The main research results are as follows :1. By developing an improved expansion test device, the relationship between the expansion force and the expansion rate and the variation of moisture content was obtained. After the expansion force reached the maximum value, the expansion force of weakly expanding soil did not increase with the increase of moisture content, while the expansion force of strongly expanding soil decreased sharply with the continuous increase of moisture content. The expansion rate presents three stages: rapid, slow and stable expansion as the moisture content increases. Based on its test curve, the law of the coefficient of expansion and moisture content was obtained, and the equations of expansion force and expansion rate were derived, providing effective assistance for engineering practice. 2. By developing an improved test device for measuring shrinkage with a porous scale plate, the relationship between shrinkage deformation and moisture content was obtained. It was concluded that the shrinkage of expansive soil goes through three stages: rapid, slow and stable shrinkage, and the time to reach each stage is approximately the same. The vertical shrinkage value is greater than the radial shrinkage value. The reasons for water loss shrinkage in each stage were analyzed. Based on the test curve, the relationship between the shrinkage coefficient and the moisture content was obtained, and the linear shrinkage and volume shrinkage equations were derived. The relationship between the substrate suction and moisture content was obtained by the filter paper method, and it was concluded that the shrinkage deformation is closely related to the substrate suction. 3. Through the developed π -ruler test device for measuring the expansion and contraction cycle, by comparing the two test schemes of contracting first and then expanding and expanding first and then contracting, it was concluded that the expansion and contraction deformation law of contracting first and then expanding is the same as that of the traditional test device, but the relationship between the expansion and contraction deformation and the initial moisture content shows an opposite law. The test of expansion first and then contraction shows that the expansion deformation is consistent with the test results of the traditional device, but the contraction deformation law is different from the traditional test law. This experiment still needs further optimization and exploration. 4. By comparing the expansion and contraction characteristics, it is concluded that the essence of the expansion and contraction deformation is caused by the change of soil moisture. The fine microscopic change process of the expansion and contraction deformation of expansive soil is analyzed respectively through the expansion and contraction mechanism and the driving force of expansion and contraction. The rationality and shortcomings of the lattice expansion theory are concluded. Compared with the lattice expansion theory, the double-layer theory can more comprehensively and fully explain the expansion and contraction mechanism.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: This paper takes the expansive soil in Nanning, Guangxi as the research object, and aims to study the dry-wet cycling effect and microscopic mechanism of expansive soil under atmospheric action. Combined with the on-site observation data of expansive soil slopes, through indoor expansive soil strength tests, deformation tests and microscopic tests, The variation laws of the strength and deformation of expansive soil under dry-wet cycling conditions have been comprehensively revealed, and the microscopic mechanism of the dry-wet cycling effect has been analyzed. The main research results are as follows: 1. Within the range of atmospheric influence depth, combined with the on-site moisture content observation data, the dry-wet cycle amplitude of expansive soil is determined. Through indoor conventional triaxial and direct shear tests, the variation law of the strength of expansive soil under dry-wet cycling conditions was studied, and the corresponding strength attenuation formula was established. On this basis, the relationship between the strength of expansive soil and its moisture content as well as the cycle range under dry-wet cycling conditions was discussed. 2 The expansion and contraction tests of expansive soil were carried out to study the variation laws of the expansion and contraction deformation of expansive soil under dry-wet cycling conditions, and the corresponding relationship formulas were established. On this basis, the relationship between the expansion and contraction indices of expansive soil and the moisture content under dry-wet cycling conditions was discussed. 3 The pore size distribution curves during the dry-wet cycling process were determined by mercury pressure test and corrected analysis was carried out. The soil-water characteristic curve of the dry-wet cycling process of expansive soil was calculated by using the corrected pore size distribution curve, and its variation law was analyzed. Based on this, the variation law of the substrate suction force during the dry-wet cycle was studied, and the corresponding relationship was established. Finally, the generation mechanism of the dry-wet cycle effect of expansive soil was expounded from the aspects of microstructure (matrix suction and fissure). 4 Based on the experimental results, the stability analysis of expansive soil slopes considering the dry-wet cycling effect was carried out, and the necessity and feasibility of the dry-wet cycling stratification theory of expansive soil slopes were expounded.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: The engineering problem of expansive soil is a major technical challenge encountered in the construction of expressways in China. Combining practical projects, studying the engineering characteristics and treatment techniques of expansive soil has significant theoretical significance and engineering application value. Based on practical engineering, a comprehensive study on the engineering properties of expansive soil is conducted by means of indoor tests, field tests, in-situ monitoring, and a combination of theoretical analysis and numerical simulation. This further explores the analysis methods and treatment countermeasures of expansive soil, providing a scientific basis for engineering construction in expansive soil areas. At the same time, it promotes the development of the discipline of expansive soil and unsaturated soil mechanics. The main research contents and achievements of this paper are as follows: 1. A survey on the diseases of expansive soil on expressways was carried out, revealing the characteristics and causes of the diseases of expansive soil on highways, deepening the understanding of the hazards of expansive soil, and providing guidance for the formulation of expansive soil disposal plans. 2. An in-depth discussion was conducted on the indicators that reflect and characterize the expansion and contraction mechanism and characteristics of expansive soil, including the liquid limit, plasticity index, free expansion rate, and <0.005, which can fully reflect and characterize the expansion and contraction mechanism and characteristics of expansive soil Five indicators, including the content of mm particles and the total rate of expansion and contraction, were used as the discrimination indicators for expansive soil. A new method for the discrimination and classification of expansive soil - the fuzzy comprehensive evaluation method for the discrimination and classification of expansive soil - was established and verified through experiments. The adoption of this method will make the evaluation results as objective as possible, thereby achieving better practical effects. 3. Systematic experimental research on the engineering characteristics of expansive soil was carried out, comprehensively expounding the variation laws and influencing factors of the compaction characteristics, expansion and contraction characteristics, and strength and deformation characteristics of expansive soil, deepening the understanding of the engineering characteristics of expansive soil. 4. In view of the characteristics of infiltration in expansive soil slopes, a new simplified equivalent model for the analysis of infiltration in expansive soil that can take fractures into account was established, and an effective approach for analyzing the infiltration of soil fractures was obtained. The simplified equivalent model divides the soil slope into two layers based on the variation of the weathering degree of the slope soil with depth. The first layer is the strongly weathered layer. The selection of the water movement parameters of the soil in this layer should take into account the increase in soil permeability caused by the existence of fractures. The second layer is an unweathered layer and can be regarded as the nature of the undisturbed soil. The water movement parameters adopt the corresponding parameters of the undisturbed soil. A numerical simulation study on rainfall infiltration of expansive soil slopes was carried out. The numerical simulation shows that the water movement of expansive soil slopes under rainfall conditions mainly occurs within the strongly weathered layer. 5. In view of the deformation and failure characteristics of expansive soil slopes, a stratified treatment method for the stability analysis of expansive soil slopes is proposed. The specific method is to divide the slope into the weathered layer and the unweathered layer. The thickness of the weathered layer is determined according to the depth of atmospheric influence, and the residual strength value is selected as the strength parameter. Then, the slope stability analysis is carried out by the limit equilibrium method. Numerical simulation analysis of the stability of expansive soil slopes was carried out. The numerical simulation shows that the stability of expansive soil slopes is mainly controlled by the shallow layer model. 6. A stability analysis method for unsaturated soil slopes that can consider water infiltration was established by using the limit equilibrium analysis method. This method takes into account that the moisture content of the soil slope after rainfall is a distribution field, and the shear strength parameter is a function of saturation. A prediction and forecast model for soil slope stability under rainfall conditions is proposed. The specific idea is as follows: Firstly, the seepage field is calculated by numerical methods. Then, the strength distribution of the entire field is deduced by using the strength theory of unsaturated soil. Subsequently, the slope stability is analyzed by using the limit equilibrium analysis method or the finite element method to seek the correlation between the slope stability safety factor and the rainfall characteristic parameters, such as This can be used to predict the stability of soil slopes based on weather forecasts. The proposal of this method provides a new idea for the stability prediction and early warning of expansive soil slopes under climatic conditions, and offers a scientific basis for the effective treatment of expansive soil slopes. 7. A successful artificial simulation test of 'prolonged drought and heavy rain' inducing expansive soil landslides was conducted, which reproduced the most common shallow sliding phenomenon in the instability of expansive soil slopes and revealed the inherent law of expansive soil landslides triggered by rainfall. Based on the above-mentioned experimental research, theoretical analysis and numerical simulation, research on the treatment technology of expansive soil was carried out. The structural form of the expansive soil embankment was suggested, the filling control standards for expansive soil were given, and a comprehensive slope protection scheme combining skeleton protection and plant protection was proposed. The relevant research results have been successfully applied in Xiangjing Expressway, Hefei-Liuzhou-Ye Expressway, Liuzhou Expressway, New Hankou Airport and 2319-II Project, achieving good technical and economic effects and having a promising prospect for promotion and application.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: Expansive soil is a special type of cohesive soil. China is one of the countries with the most extensive distribution of expansive soil. With the large-scale construction of high-grade highways in China and the accelerated pace of development in the western region, conducting research on the subgrade and slope issues of highways in expansive soil areas has significant practical significance. This paper focuses on the classification and evaluation methods of expansive soil, as well as the settlement and slope stability issues of expansive soil subgrades by means of theoretical analysis, experimental analysis and numerical analysis. The main contents are as follows: Firstly, through the experiments and investigations of expansive soil in Yichang area, the characteristics of the composition, structure and physical properties of expansive soil, as well as the expansion and contraction characteristics and subgrade diseases were analyzed; The reasons for the charging of expansive soil particles and the conditions of cation exchange were analyzed; The mechanism of the expansion and contraction of expansive soil was discussed; Experimental research shows that expansive soil after dry-wet cycling has strength attenuation. Secondly, based on the experiments and achievements of expansive soil in Yichang area, the plastic graph method, multi-index evaluation method and multi-variable linear function evaluation method suitable for the discrimination and classification of expansive soil in this area were proposed, providing a reference for the evaluation of regional expansive soil. The fuzzy mathematics comprehensive evaluation method was proposed. The sensitivity of the results of the fuzzy comprehensive evaluation of expansive soil to the weight vector was discussed. By comparing its evaluation results with those of other methods such as the normative method, it was indicated that this method is a relatively safe and accurate evaluation method. Thirdly, the settlement characteristics and the law of expansion and contraction settlement of expansive soil subgrade under load were analyzed. The basic mechanical conditions for the expansion and contraction settlement of expansive soil subgrades were studied. The limit equilibrium method for determining the atmospheric influence depth of expansive soil was proposed; The 'incremental method' for analyzing the settlement of expansive soil subgrades was proposed, and the corresponding analysis model was given. The calculation expression for the instantaneous settlement of the roadbed was derived. The calculation formula for the expansion and contraction settlement that could take into account factors such as the direction and incomplete reversibility of the expansion and contraction settlement was proposed, and the settlement of the expansive soil subgrade of the Yihuang Highway was analyzed by using the increment method. By using the incremental method, the relationship between the settlement of expansive soil subgrade with time, stress path and moisture content can be expressed, laying a foundation for further simulation and analysis of the settlement process of expansive soil subgrade. Fourth, a limit state equation for the settlement of expansive soil subgrade that can simultaneously reflect the directionality of expansion and contraction settlement and main consolidation settlement has been established. By using the 'JC' method, a series of calculation formulas for the reliability index of expansive soil subgrade settlement were derived, and an iterative program for solving the reliability index was given. The reliability and sensitivity analysis of specific subgrades show that the failure of settlement of expansive soil subgrades generally occurs when the soil absorbs water and expands, and its reliability decreases with the increase of the expansion level. The amount of expansion and contraction settlement accounts for a significant proportion of the total settlement of expansive soil subgrade. The reliability of subgrade settlement is highly sensitive to the value of the expansion settlement coefficient ψep, but not sensitive to the calculation depth of the main consolidation settlement. Taking the depth of atmospheric influence as the calculation depth of subgrade settlement, the calculation accuracy can be satisfied. Fifth, on the basis of the traditional concept of reliability, the concepts of 'tolerance' and 'fuzzy critical interval' were introduced, and the 'fuzzy limit state' of settlement of expansive soil subgrade and its relationship with reliability were defined. The relationship between tolerance and the fuzzy critical interval as well as the issue of the value of the fuzzy critical interval were discussed. The calculation formula for the fuzzy failure probability of expansive soil subgrade settlement was derived, and the analysis program was given. The analysis of the calculation examples shows that the fuzzy reliability of the settlement of expansive soil subgrades decreases with the increase of the expansion grade, and is very sensitive to the size of the fuzzy critical interval. This indicates that expansive soil subgrades designed according to the bearing capacity principle, especially strongly expansive soil subgrades, may be unsafe in terms of settlement. Sixth, the failure forms and characteristics of expansive soil subgrade slopes were summarized; The action characteristics of the expansive force within the expansive soil subgrade slope were analyzed, and the load models of the expansive soil embankment and cutting slope including the expansive force were established. The definition of the slope safety factor based on the strength reduction theory and the determination method of the slope failure state are given; The stability analysis of the edge slope of the expansive soil embankment of Yihuang Highway was carried out by using the strength-reduced elastoplastic finite element method and with the aid of ANSYS software. The results show that the plastic zone of the edge slope of the expansive soil road embankment passes through the foot of the slope and gradually develops towards the top of the slope within a certain depth. The most dangerous failure surface of the slope is the approximately arc-shaped sliding zone with a certain width. Finally, the stress release problem during the excavation process of expansive soil cutting slopes and the instability criterion problem in the slope simulation process were discussed, and the comprehensive discrimination basis for slope instability was proposed. Dynamic simulation analyses were conducted on the stability of expansive soil cutting slopes during the excavation process, before and after water immersion, and at different water immersion depths. The analysis results show that as the excavation deepens or the water immersion depth of the slope increases, the safety factor of the expansive soil cutting slope continuously decreases. It indicates that the stability of the expansive soil cutting slope is directly controlled by the excavation effect during the construction stage and is largely controlled by the immersion and wetting effect after the work. Therefore, to enhance its stability, the focus should be on doing a good job in slope protection and implementing waterproofing and drainage measures.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: Expansion and contraction are the most prominent characteristics of expansive soil. Currently, there are relatively few comparative studies on the expansion and contraction properties of expansive soil. Most of the literature focuses on the expansion (or contraction) of expansive soil alone. In addition, in engineering design, the expansion property of expansive soil is often considered, while the contraction property is frequently overlooked. Therefore, it is extremely necessary to conduct a comparative study on the expansion and contraction mechanism of expansive soil and the relationship between the two. Based on theoretical analysis, the relevant equations for the expansion and contraction of expansive soil were derived. Through a series of indoor experimental studies, the processes of water absorption expansion and water loss shrinkage of expansive soil and some related laws were clarified. Finally, by comprehensively comparing the expansion and contraction characteristics of expansive soil, the inherent unity and differences between the two were revealed, and their respective engineering significance was expounded. Firstly, the theoretical analysis section mainly discusses the expansion (contraction) process and the expansion (contraction) equation. The expansion process mainly includes two major stages: pore filling and particle expansion. Based on the theories of 'intergranular expansion (double electric layer)'' and 'lattice expansion', the theoretical expansion equation of expansive soil was established. The shrinkage process mainly involves stages such as water evaporation, water migration and fissure generation. Through the analysis of the existing shrinkage curves, the empirical shrinkage equation was obtained, and it was pointed out that the mesoscopic driving force of shrinkage comes from the liquid bridge force, and the macroscopic force is manifested as the matrix suction force. Secondly, indoor tests mainly include basic geotechnical tests, expansion tests and contraction tests. Each test reveals the relevant laws of expansion and contraction of expansive soil. The expansion test shows that the expansion process of expansive soil can be divided into three stages on the expansion time history curve: rapid expansion, decelerated expansion and slow expansion. The maximum expansion rate (force) of the sample gradually decreases with the increase of the initial moisture content, while the maximum expansion force of the sample gradually increases with the increase of the initial dry density. The entire process of water absorption and swelling of expansive soil is divided into two stages: 'pore filling' and 'particle swelling'. The 'particle swelling' can be further subdivided into 'water absorption stage - swelling stage - stabilization stage', and the water absorption and swelling equation of expansive soil has been derived. The shrinkage test shows that the evaporation process of water in the soil can be divided into three stages on the shrinkage time history curve: rapid evaporation, decelerated evaporation and slow evaporation, and the maximum linear shrinkage rate of expansive soil increases with the increase of the initial moisture content of the sample. The entire shrinkage process of expansive soil can be divided into three stages: 'rapid shrinkage, decelerated shrinkage, and residual shrinkage'. Among them, the 'rapid shrinkage stage' mainly involves the loss of pore water, the 'decelerated shrinkage stage' mainly involves the dissipation of residual pore water and weakly bound water, and the 'residual shrinkage stage' mainly involves the diffusion of water vapor. The force causing the soil to contract is the matrix suction force, and the maximum value measured in the test is nearly 1MPa. The indoor tests were conducted using two types of expansive soil from Chengdu and Weifang areas, and the test results of the two types of soil were basically consistent. Finally, by comparing the expansion and contraction characteristics of expansive soil, it is concluded that the unity between the two lies in the fact that the change in soil structure is essentially caused by moisture variation. Based on this viewpoint, the equilibrium moisture content of the expansive soil moisture retention method is obtained. The differences between the expansibility and shrinkage of expansive soil mainly focus on the state of water, the direction of water movement, the transformation of soil structure and the change of soil strength, etc. Through the comparison of these differences, the deformation and failure mechanism of expansive soil slopes can be explained.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: Expansive soil, as a special type of soil widely distributed around the world, has caused significant damage and impact on engineering construction in related regions and has always been one of the difficult problems in the field of geotechnical engineering research. Shallow instability failure is the most common and frequent failure phenomenon of expansive soil slopes in railway, highway, water conservancy and other projects. However, at present, there is a lack of sufficient understanding of the mechanism of shallow instability failure. The traditional limit equilibrium stability analysis method cannot truthfully reflect and explain the true characteristics of such failures of expansive soil slopes, and also restricts how to take reasonable and effective treatment measures in engineering construction Solve the engineering problems caused by its special nature. Therefore, in-depth research on the shallow instability failure mechanism of expansive soil slopes and, on this basis, exploration of corresponding engineering measures are the keys to ensuring the safety and economy of engineering design. This paper, in combination with the stability problem of expansive soil slopes in the Middle Route Project of the South-to-North Water Diversion Project, conducts in-depth research on the most essential engineering characteristic of expansive soil - its expansibility. Through large-scale static physical model tests, it studies the occurrence, development and failure of slope expansion deformation and sliding caused by water infiltration in compacted expansive soil slopes without the influence of factors such as fissures, over-consolidation, and dry-wet cycles Cheng Based on the theory of 'humidity stress field, a multi-field coupling numerical analysis method of unsaturated seepage field - expansion deformation field - stress field is proposed. Combined with the results of model tests and numerical analysis, the failure mechanism of shallow instability of expansive soil slopes is expounded. Based on this, starting from the stiff-soil interaction mechanism of reinforced soil, the restraint effect of geogrids on the expansion deformation of expansive soil and their improvement effect on the shallow stability of slopes are studied, providing a theoretical basis for determining reasonable engineering treatment measures for expansive soil. Through the research of this paper, the main conclusions are as follows: (1) For the extremely common shallow instability failure form of expansive soil slopes, a large-scale physical model test (6m×2m×2.8m) was adopted, and combined with the coupled numerical simulation analysis of the humidity field - expansive deformation field - stress field, it was revealed that the essence of shallow instability of expansive soil slopes lies in the expansibility of expansive soil. The mechanism of shallow progressive instability failure of expansive soil slopes controlled by expansion deformation can be expounded as follows: When expansive soil slopes are subjected to the action of external hydraulic boundaries, it first leads to an uneven temporal and spatial distribution of the water content field within the slope, subsequently causing uneven expansion deformation of the soil mass, redistribution of the stress-strain field, and the formation of a shear stress concentration area at the junction of unsaturated and saturated infiltration, resulting in local shear failure, which gradually extends to the deep part of the slope and eventually forms multiple shear surfaces, leading to slope collapse It causes progressive instability and damage. (2) For the stability analysis of expansive soil slopes, based on the 'humidity-stress field' theory, a finite element numerical analysis method combining the multi-field coupling effects of unsaturated and saturated seepage fields, expansive deformation fields, and stress fields is proposed. Through simulation of model tests, it is proved that this multi-field coupling analysis model and method can truly reflect the shallow progressive instability of expansive soil slopes This process provides a useful analytical method for a deeper understanding of the failure mechanism of the shallow stability of slopes. (3) Based on the similarity of temperature expansion and humidity expansion, the thermally solid coupling finite element numerical analysis method was adopted. According to the test results of indoor expansion of expansive soil under no-load and loaded conditions, the variation laws of the expansion coefficient under different moisture contents, dry densities and loads were inverted. The results show that the coefficient of expansion is comprehensively affected by factors such as moisture content, dry density and load. Under low load conditions, the moisture content and the coefficient of expansion approximately conform to a linear relationship. Therefore, in the multi-field coupling numerical calculation, the moisture content - expansion coefficient (saturation - expansion coefficient) relationship corresponding to the low load condition is adopted to reflect the variation law of the expansion coefficient caused by the humidity change at different parts of the slope. (4) A large-scale expansion model test was conducted to systematically study the development law of moisture absorption expansion deformation of expansive soil after laying geogrids. The influence laws of different geogrid parameters (strength, type, number of laying layers and method, etc.) on expansion deformation were obtained, and the feasibility of geogrids in suppressing expansion deformation was evaluated. (5) Through large-scale physical model tests, the effect of geogrids on the shallow stability of expansive soil slopes was studied, providing a basic experimental basis for the engineering treatment measures of expansive soil.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: With the large-scale construction of buildings in the expansive soil area of Chengdu, buildings are getting taller and taller, basements are getting deeper and deeper, and the depth of foundation pits excavated in the expansive soil area is getting larger and larger. However, cracking and collapse accidents of foundation pit slopes still occur continuously. The stability of expansive soil slopes in the Chengdu area plays a dominant role in factors such as engineering safety and economic benefits in the expansive soil area of Chengdu. This research topic, in combination with the foundation pit engineering construction of the Longtan Temple Science and Technology Innovation Center project and the Wangjiang Jinyuan project, conducts experimental research on the physical and mechanical properties and engineering characteristics of expansive soil in Chengdu. The main contents are as follows: (1) It introduces the distribution characteristics, genesis types, material composition and structural features of expansive soil in the Chengdu area. The typical expansive soil of the Longtan Temple Science and Technology Innovation Center project in Chengdu area was selected for indoor tests on relevant physical and mechanical properties and engineering characteristics. Among them, the yellow clay in the expansive soil of this area and the grayish-white clay in the fissures were mainly tested respectively to determine the most unfavorable relevant parameter indicators for their stability. (2) On-site direct shear tests were conducted on typical expansive soil from the Longtan Temple Science and Technology Innovation Center project and the Wangjiang Jingyuan project in the Chengdu area by using the large shear instrument method and the one-time horizontal shear method. Through on-site large shear and thrust shear tests on expansive soil in its natural state, saturated state, different depths and strongly weathered mudstone at the same position in the Chengdu area, the shear strength indicators of expansive soil and strongly weathered mudstone in this area were obtained. Meanwhile, the shear surfaces of the samples were described accordingly to determine the failure mode and characteristics of expansive soil in this area. (3) By summarizing the relevant test data of the shear strength indicators of indoor and outdoor tests and conducting comparative analysis, the relationship between different shear strength indicators measured in indoor tests, outdoor tests, and indoor and outdoor tests under different conditions and sampling methods was analyzed and studied. At the same time, the main factors affecting the strength of expansive soil in this area were also analyzed, and the recommended values of the shear strength indicators were determined.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: In the current engineering practice of expansive soil, such as embankment filling and slope design, reconstituted soil is often involved. Therefore, studying the expansion and contraction grades, expansion and contraction characteristics, and strength characteristics of reconstituted expansive soil is of great significance for the future construction of highways and railways, prevention and treatment of subgrade diseases, and slope engineering design in expansive land areas. In this paper, a series of experimental studies on the expansion and contraction characteristics and strength characteristics of remolded expansive soil were conducted respectively by using a portable consolidation instrument, a compression instrument and a conventional direct shear instrument. The following achievements have been made, which are summarized as follows: 1 The existing classification methods of expansive soil expansion and contraction at home and abroad are reviewed. Through the comparison of the existing classification methods of expansive soil, the advantages and disadvantages of various classification methods of expansive soil are pointed out. Meanwhile, through indoor tests and theoretical calculations, the expansive soil in the sections of Changde-Zhangjiajie Expressway and Nanning-Youyiguan Expressway was reclassified respectively by using the fuzzy mathematics discrimination method and the standard moisture absorption and moisture content discrimination method, providing a reference basis for future engineering construction in these areas. 2 Based on a large number of indoor tests of expansive soil in Youyiguan, Nanjing, the characteristics of the expansion and contraction time history curves of expansive soil were studied to summarize the expansion and contraction mechanism and influencing factors of reconstructed expansive soil. The similarities and differences between the expansion and contraction curves of expansive soil were sought, and the quantitative relationships between the initial moisture content, dry density, external pressure, etc. of the soil and the expansion deformation and expansion force were explored. 3 Through direct shear tests of reshaped expansive soil with different dry densities and initial moisture contents under different normal stresses, the stress-strain characteristics and changes in strength indicators of the expansion of Nanyou Road were studied. The variation characteristics of the peak strength and residual strength of reshaped expansive soil with initial conditions (dry density, initial moisture content, overburden pressure) were fully understood. The quantitative relationships among cohesion, internal friction Angle, initial moisture content, dry density and the number of dry-wet cycles were established. 4 Through the analogy test of the engineering properties of red clay with swelling and shrinking properties and expansive soil, it is believed that although red clay with swelling and shrinking properties and expansive soil have similarities, due to the differences in material composition and microstructure, there are many differences in their engineering properties. In engineering practice, they should be treated differently. 5 On-site monitoring and finite element simulation analysis of the deformation characteristics of expansive soil subgrade during the construction process were conducted to study the variations of vertical settlement and lateral displacement of expansive soil subgrade with the height of subgrade fill, identify the deformation laws, and use them to guide the subgrade construction of future expansive land sections.

sub-categories: expansion and contraction of expansive soil
text: The selection of discrimination and classification indicators and methods for expansive soil has a profound impact on the formulation of engineering measures. Choosing reasonable indicators and classification methods is of vital importance. This paper, starting from these two aspects and in combination with the ongoing subgrade projects of the Hefei-Liuzhou Expressway and the Liuzhou Expressway, conducts a systematic indoor experimental study on expansive soil in the central Anhui region, including mineral composition, physicochemical analysis tests, basic physical property tests, strength characteristic tests, compaction characteristic tests, etc., to investigate the mineral composition, basic physical properties, strength characteristics, and expansion and contraction of expansive soil in the central Anhui region The properties of several aspects such as sexuality have been elaborated in detail. Several commonly used indicators characterizing the expansion and contraction mechanism of expansive soil, such as free expansion rate, liquid plastic limit, plasticity index, standard moisture absorption content, and colloidal particle content, were systematically studied and discussed. Combined with statistical theory, the sensitivity of the above indicators was analyzed. The research found that the plasticity index has the greatest impact on the discrimination and classification results of expansive soil. An in-depth study was conducted on the rationality of the test methods and indicators for the free expansion rate. In the test methods, the influence of soil sample preparation on the free expansion rate was mainly studied. Through experimental analysis and research, the defects existing in the free expansion rate test were pointed out, and reasonable test methods were suggested. By using the recommended test method, a large number of tests were conducted on expansive soil in the central Anhui region. The extent to which the index reflects the essence of expansive soil was studied, the correlation between this index and other indicators was investigated, the characteristics of free expansion rate in the central Anhui region were studied, and the classification features of free expansion rate were investigated. Through comparison with the indicators reflecting the essential characteristics such as montmorillonite content and plasticity index, the current regulations were discovered The classification standard recommended by Fan is not applicable to the discrimination and classification of expansive soil in the central Anhui region. A reasonable classification standard was suggested and its rationality was verified. For the recommended indicator of the new highway code - the standard moisture absorption content, its physical significance and test methods are briefly expounded. The degree to which the indicator reflects the essence of expansive soil is studied. The correlation between this indicator and other indirect discrimination indicators is investigated. The characteristics of the standard moisture absorption content in the central Anhui region are studied. The statistical and classification characteristics of the standard moisture absorption content are investigated. Through the reflection of the essence characteristics By comparing the indicators such as montmorillonite content and plasticity index, it was found that the classification standards recommended by the norms were not applicable to the discrimination and classification of expansive soil in the central Anhui region. A reasonable classification standard was suggested, and the rationality of the classification standard was verified. A new criterion for identifying expansive soil - the free expansion ratio - has been introduced. It is the volume ratio of 10g of dried soil sample when it is stably deposited in standard graduated cylinders (usually 50 mL) filled with distilled water and diesel (or CCl4) respectively. The physical significance and advantages of this index were expounded. By collecting soil samples and conducting correlation analyses with essential indicators such as montmorillonite content, specific surface area, and cation exchange capacity, as well as indirect discrimination indicators such as free expansion rate, plasticity index, liquid limit, and standard moisture absorption content, and other comparative analyses, the rationality of the new index was verified. Combining the advantages of short period and easy operation in the free expansion rate test method, a reasonable test method is suggested. The classification standard of the free expansion ratio was recommended and its rationality was preliminarily verified. A new statistical discriminant classification method - Fisher discriminant analysis method - was proposed. The content of montmorillonite is controlled
